name: 360 Credit Consulting
description_short: Colorado-based credit repair firm offering dispute-based credit restoration, free credit analysis, and consumer education through a certified FICO Credit Team.
description_long:
360 Credit Consulting is a credit repair and restoration firm based in Broomfield, Colorado. The company operates with what it describes as a "certified FICO Credit Team," indicating staff trained in FICO scoring methodology. Their program is built on consumer law research and credit bureau reporting practices. They are not a lender, credit union, or nonprofit counseling agency — they are a for-profit credit repair service operating in the dispute-based restoration space.

The company's core offering is identifying and disputing errors, inaccuracies, and unverifiable items on clients' credit reports. Their intake model begins with a free credit analysis, during which clients receive a personalized review of their credit situation along with realistic timeline projections. From there, they engage in credit restoration work — the formal dispute process — and credit approval assistance aimed at helping clients qualify for loans and better interest rates. Supplemental credit education resources are provided at no charge as part of the engagement.

With a 4.7-star Google rating drawn from over 950 reviews, 360 Credit Consulting has built a notably strong reputation for a regional credit repair firm. Client reviews reference individual agents by name — Jessica and Albert Sollesa among them — pointing to an agent-assigned, relationship-driven service model rather than an automated or bulk-dispute pipeline. The appointment-based consultation process, complete with SMS reminders, further signals a structured and personalized approach to client onboarding.

The company's most significant limitation is pricing opacity: no fees, monthly costs, or service tiers are disclosed on their website, so consumers must engage through the free consultation before understanding the financial commitment. No money-back guarantee was found in the research. Standard credit repair caveats also apply — nothing 360 Credit Consulting can do is legally beyond what a consumer can pursue independently through direct bureau dispute channels. That said, for consumers who want professional guidance, named agent accountability, and a hands-off dispute process backed by a high review volume, 360 Credit Consulting is a credible regional option.
pros: 4.7/5 Google rating from 952+ reviews — unusually high review volume for a regional credit repair firm
Agent-assigned service model with clients naming specific advisors (e.g., Jessica, Albert Sollesa) — not an anonymous automated process
Free credit analysis with personalized review and realistic timeline projections before any commitment
Certified FICO Credit Team — staff credentialed in FICO scoring methodology
Appointment-based model with SMS reminders suggests an organized, structured client experience
Consumer law-based dispute approach grounded in credit bureau reporting methods rather than generic template disputes
Appears to double as credit education resource — free educational content included in engagement
cons: No pricing published on website — consumers cannot compare costs without going through the consultation funnel
No money-back guarantee found anywhere on website
No mobile app or client portal mentioned — unclear how clients track dispute progress
No NFCC, HUD, or CDFI certifications — not a nonprofit or government-approved counseling agency
Company founding year and corporate history not disclosed publicly
best_for: Consumers with documentable errors or inaccuracies on their credit reports who want professional dispute help
People who need credit score improvement to qualify for a mortgage, auto loan, or better interest rates
Individuals who prefer a named, relationship-based credit specialist over an automated dispute software service
diagnosis: 360 Credit Consulting is best suited for consumers with verifiable errors on their credit reports who want a hands-on, agent-guided dispute process backed by a strong local reputation. The lack of published pricing is a real barrier — prospective clients should use the free analysis to understand costs before committing. Not a fit for consumers seeking debt settlement, nonprofit financial counseling, or any loan product.
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
